pdfinfo_summary: pdfinfo reports a non-encrypted 9-page optimized PDF 1.6 with letter-size pages, no metadata stream, no JavaScript, and an AcroForm catalog.
text_layer_probe: pdftotext returned zero characters and mutool draw -F txt returned zero characters on the captured DOJ-linked PDF, so the local artifact does not expose an extractable text layer on this host.
image_structure_probe: strings plus object inspection showed each page content stream as image placement (`/Im0 Do`) rather than text operators; pages 1-8 are 1-bit DeviceGray JBIG2 images at 2552x3300 and page 9 is a FlateDecode RGB image at 1224x1584.
case_number_relevance: Supports a narrower reviewer-safe conclusion that the unread suffix after `25 Cr.` is consistent with an image-only source capture rather than a missed plain-text extraction route.
blocker_interpretation: The remaining caption blocker is best treated as source-image legibility / alternate-source availability friction, not as a simple failure to try standard text extractors.
paper_thesis_use: Feeds methods, limitations, and reproducibility notes for why the Nate row remains charging-stage plus partial-caption normalization only.
observatory_hook: Document-processing barrier facet for court-family rows: image-only official PDFs vs text-extractable official PDFs
